DATP Public Comment reading room is live. The City of Colorado Springs Digital Accessibility Transition Plan (Draft 2026), with the full plan embedded as a PDF and twelve structured public comments residents can adapt and submit. Comment portal links live in the reading room.
Public comment · COS Digital Accessibility Transition Plan · May 2026
Open the DATP reading room →
BOCC petition — service plan transparency & algorithmic pricing disclosure. A general resident petition with four narrow asks within existing BOCC authority. Sign and send.
Civic action · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Open petition letter →
Veterans' petition to the EPC BOCC. Companion petition for veterans of any branch — service plan transparency and algorithmic pricing protections, framed around military housing harms.
Civic action · Veterans · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Open veterans' petition →
LDC update — public comment on service plan transparency. A 300-word comment ready to submit through the El Paso County Land Development Code update process.
Public comment · LDC update · El Paso County · May 2026
Open LDC comment →
Background brief — algorithmic rent pricing in El Paso County. Sourced reference for journalists, policy analysts, and the COS Chamber housing committee: federal antitrust action, local policy options, and the case for BOCC-level service plan transparency.
Reference · Housing & pricing · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Read background brief →
HOME-ARP comment window is live — closes May 26, 2026. COS is collecting public comment on the HOME-ARP Allocation Plan amendment via an ArcGIS Survey123 form Esri's own ACR rates as "Partially Supports" on WCAG 2.1 A/AA. A WCAG 2.1 AA Drupal Webform replacement is ready to install — bilingual EN/ES, JSON webhook + staff email + native storage, inline accommodation intake.

ADA Title II deadline: April 24, 2026 — 16 days. All local government websites must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Zero district sites are confirmed compliant on PDF documents.
HB21-1110 · 28 C.F.R. Part 35 · April 8, 2026
Open district audit dashboard →
249 of 284 metro districts have no website. CORA request is the only way a resident can access their own district's records.
EPC Assessor audit · April 2026
See district audit →
WS Districts (WSDM) serves 30+ districts on a single WordPress site. Last calendar entry: 2021. No governance docs. No financials. One compliance letter fixes all 30+ simultaneously.
Platform audit · April 2026
Draft compliance letter →
High priority
All posted PDFs likely fail WCAG 1.3.1. Meeting minutes, budgets, and audit docs are almost certainly untagged. This is the real HB21-1110 failure — not the homepage.
ADA document audit · April 2026
View ADA analysis →
0 of 8 audited sites post their service plan. C.R.S. §32-1-202 requires it. This is the foundational legal document. Not one district links it publicly.
Governance gap · §32-1-202
See audit dashboard →
Annual audit documents: publicly posted by 0 of 8 districts. Required by §32-1-106. Due to State Auditor by July 31. The clock is running.

See audit dashboard →
Informational
2 CORA requests pending response. Military housing numbers and district governance records both awaiting reply. Response window is still open.

View tracker →
Wolfersberger compliance and Bellamese analysis complete. New findings ready for review on developer agreements, compliance history, and parcel-level records.

Read analysis →
Jurisdiction finder is live. Enter any El Paso County address to find your metro districts, school district, city jurisdiction, and utilities — plain language, no login.
New · April 2026
Find my jurisdiction →
CSPD Blotter — Weekly History is live. 271 incidents pulled directly from the live blotter feed, paired with a Poisson 95% confidence band to flag statistical anomalies week to week. Tabs are full APG-pattern keyboard accessible; every chart has an aria-label.
New · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open blotter dashboard →
Lorson Ranch financial dashboard is live. Seven cross-pledged metro districts, $94.2M aggregate audited deficit, IGA money flows, and a per-home tax calculator — sourced from 2026 budgets and 2024 audits. Companion plain-language briefing available as a download.
New · Special Districts · April 2026
Open Lorson Ranch dashboard →
CBI Missing Persons form — accessible rebuild is live. Resolves all seven critical/serious findings from the ADA audit: skip link, live regions, focus management, EN/ES localization, real-time validation messaging. Drops into the existing Drupal Webform infrastructure with optional Claude API routing.
New · State · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open CBI form rebuild →
LexisNexis ADAM Program dashboard — accessible rebuild is live. Audit and reference rebuild of the missing-children dashboard: APG-pattern tabs with roving tabindex and full keyboard nav, solid amber focus rings (3:1 against any surface), darker success-green for AA contrast, and "View Poster" placeholders converted from dead anchors to real buttons.
New · Private Sector · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA

DOJ Title II Compliance Deadline 28 C.F.R. §§ 35.200–35.205 · WCAG 2.1 Level AA Required
18 days remaining
CO
AG
Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser · Department of Law
ADA Compliance Brief April 2026 · Digital Accessibility
Prepared for AG Office Review

Emergency Compliance Package · Colorado Dept. of Law

Digital Accessibility
Compliance Brief

A comprehensive ADA / WCAG 2.1 Level AA audit of the Colorado Attorney General's digital complaint infrastructure identified 95 confirmed violations across four technology platforms. The DOJ Title II compliance deadline of April 24, 2026 — enacted by 28 C.F.R. §§ 35.200–35.205 — is 18 days from the date of this brief. This package contains the full audit record, compliant form prototype, and remediation roadmap required to demonstrate good-faith compliance and reduce enforcement exposure.

95
Confirmed
WCAG Violations
37
Critical
Severity
4
Technology
Platforms
$115K
DOJ First
Violation Fine
$815K
Max Precedent
Total Exposure
18
Days to
Deadline
Mandatory Compliance Notice — Attorney General's Office of Colorado

The Colorado Attorney General's Office, as a state government entity, is legally obligated under Title II of the Americans with Disabilities Act (42 U.S.C. §§ 12131–12165) and the Department of Justice's Web Accessibility Final Rule (28 C.F.R. §§ 35.200–35.205, effective June 24, 2024) to ensure that all public-facing websites, web applications, and complaint intake systems conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA by no later than April 24, 2026. The Colorado AG's office serves a population well exceeding 50,000 persons, placing it squarely within the mandatory compliance cohort specified at 28 C.F.R. § 35.200(b)(1). The DOJ has confirmed that no extensions to this deadline are available.

Enforcement of this obligation is compounded by the fact that this office is charged with enforcing ADA Title II against other Colorado entities under 42 U.S.C. § 12133. Any failure to comply with the same standards this office enforces creates substantial institutional, legal, and reputational exposure. The DOJ Civil Rights Division enforces Title II through complaint investigations, compliance reviews, and pattern-or-practice litigation. Civil penalties for a first violation are $115,231 (inflation-adjusted 2024); a second violation is $230,464. Settlement agreements with comparable state agencies have resulted in total costs of $665,000–$815,000 inclusive of remediation, legal fees, accessibility coordinator requirements, and mandatory staff training. Emergency remediation rates — applicable to work commenced after April 24 — are currently running 4–5× normal vendor rates due to market scarcity.

Additional obligations arise under Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act (29 U.S.C. § 794), Executive Order 13166 (LEP access — directly implicated by the Spanish-language complaint infrastructure findings), Title VI of the Civil Rights Act (42 U.S.C. § 2000d), and 8 CCR 1501-11 (Colorado Technology Accessibility Standards). The materials contained in this package constitute the good-faith compliance record recommended by DOJ guidance for entities demonstrating documented remediation effort prior to enforcement action.

01

Compliance Package — Deliverables

Primary Deliverable

ADA / WCAG 2.1 Formal Audit Report

PDF · 14 sections · 30+ pages
📋

Full formal audit covering coag.gov, complaints.coag.gov (Salesforce Experience Cloud), all seven Gravity Forms deployments, the Spanish complaint infrastructure, and coloradonocall.com. Includes statutory citations on every finding, technology stack map, remediation priority matrix (top 30 findings), and platform recommendations. Formatted for submission to DOJ as a good-faith compliance record.

37 Critical 51 Serious 4 Platforms Statutory Citations
Open Report 64 KB · PDF
Technical Prototype

Salesforce-Integrated Complaint Form

HTML · Production-ready · Bilingual EN/ES
⚡

Triage-first complaint intake form modeled on the FBI tips architecture. Single narrative field — no forced self-categorization. Full Salesforce REST API integration (Case object), Claude AI routing, bilingual English/Spanish toggle with correct lang switching, and all 95 audit findings addressed in design. 131 accessibility passes, 0 failures after remediation.

131 A11y Passes Salesforce API AI Routing WCAG 2.1 AA
Open Form 74 KB · HTML
Technical Audit

Interactive ADA Audit — coag.gov

HTML · DOM-level findings · Annotated
🔍

Interactive HTML record of the live browser audit. Documents real-time DOM inspection results — tab order, focus indicators, ARIA chains, shadow DOM traversal, color contrast computations, and heading hierarchy. Complements the formal PDF with technical evidence at the code level. Useful for the development team implementing remediation.

DOM Analysis Contrast Ratios Tab Order Map Shadow DOM
Open Audit 34 KB · HTML
UX Prototype

File-a-Complaint Hub Redesign

HTML · Triage-first architecture · AG branded
✦

Full redesign of the coag.gov complaint intake hub applying the FBI tips triage model: skip link first, global focus ring, descriptive card links, correct heading hierarchy, no forced self-categorization. Demonstrates what the landing experience should be post-remediation. Addresses findings #1–#20 and #45–#48 directly.

Focus Fixed Links Descriptive Headings Correct FBI Tips Model
Open Redesign 43 KB · HTML
02

Critical Findings Requiring Immediate Action

Finding Severity Description WCAG SC Platform
#18 Critical No visible focus indicator on any link site-wide — keyboard users receive no visual feedback on any interactive element 2.4.7 coag.gov
#19 Critical All collapsed nav dropdowns remain in Tab order — 50+ invisible links precede main content for keyboard users 2.1.1, 2.4.3 coag.gov
#45–48 Critical Four complaint categories route to broken destinations — Securities Fraud has no form; others redirect to licensing pages or press releases 2.4.4 coag.gov
#24 Critical All Salesforce form field labels inaccessible — aria-labelledby chains broken by nested shadow DOM; every field announced as unlabeled to screen readers 1.3.1, 4.1.2 complaints.coag.gov
#79 Critical Spanish complaint hub has 11 nameless image-only card links — entire Spanish complaint navigation invisible to assistive technology 1.1.1, 4.1.2 coag.gov / ES
#80 Critical lang="en-US" on all Spanish-language pages — screen readers mispronounce all Spanish content; Level A (most fundamental) WCAG failure 3.1.1 (A), 3.1.2 coag.gov / ES
#92 Critical coloradonocall.com error text: 1.33:1 contrast ratio on medium-blue background — validation messages physically unreadable 1.4.3 coloradonocall.com
#25 Critical Salesforce form: focus drops to <body> after validation failure — users cannot locate error fields; no assertive announcement 3.3.1, 2.4.3 complaints.coag.gov
#35 Critical Salesforce optional demographic step: fields marked required in DOM despite being presented as optional — users cannot advance without completing them 3.3.2, 1.3.1 complaints.coag.gov
#20 Critical Skip link is not the first focusable element — announcement banner link precedes it, defeating its bypass function entirely 2.4.1 coag.gov
03

Recommended Remediation Timeline

Immediately — April 7–10, 2026
Emergency fixes: broken routes, Spanish lang, skip link
Fix four broken complaint category redirections (#45–48). Add lang="es" to all Spanish page templates (#80). Move skip link to DOM position 1 (#20). Add alt text to 11 Spanish hub card images (#79). These are content/template changes — no development sprint required. Under 28 C.F.R. § 35.130(b)(1)(i), denial of program access is the highest-priority enforcement category.
Sprint 1 — April 10–17, 2026
Global CSS fixes: focus indicators, nav tab order, paragraph spacing
Add global :focus-visible CSS rule (#18). Apply tabindex="-1" to collapsed nav dropdown links (#19). Set scroll-padding-top: 40px on html (#94). Add margin-bottom: 2em to paragraph elements (#95). These are single-file CSS changes deployable in minutes.
Sprint 2 — April 17–24, 2026 (deadline)
Gravity Forms template fixes — all 7 forms simultaneously
Add aria-hidden="true" to required asterisk spans. Change Previous button type from submit to button. Add step indicators with aria-current="step". Wrap radio groups in fieldset/legend. Add role="alert" to validation container. Template-level — fix once, applies to all 7 forms.
Post-deadline — April 25 – June 2026
Salesforce platform remediation and CRM expansion
Engage Salesforce on shadow DOM label failures (#24), CSS error dialog (#11), switch toggle ARIA (#34), and progress indicator semantics (#30). Begin Salesforce Government Cloud + Service Cloud Case Management evaluation. Deploy the triage-first complaint form prototype included in this package as replacement for the current multi-step categorization flow.
Ongoing — Q3 2026 onward
Replace Siteimprove with WAVE + AI quarterly audit cycle
Cancel Siteimprove at renewal (~$28,000/year savings). Deploy Pope Tech (WAVE-powered, ~$1,200/year) for automated regression monitoring. Schedule quarterly DOM-level AI audits to catch the 60–70% of issues automated tools cannot detect. Estimated 3-year savings: $78,600, with superior coverage.
04

Governing Legal Authority

42 U.S.C. §§ 12131–12165
ADA Title II
Prohibits discrimination on the basis of disability in services, programs, and activities of state and local governments. Primary enforcement authority for AG website accessibility.
28 C.F.R. §§ 35.200–35.205
DOJ Web Accessibility Final Rule
Effective June 24, 2024. Adopts WCAG 2.1 Level AA as the mandatory technical standard. Mandatory compliance for entities serving 50,000+ persons by April 24, 2026. No extensions.
29 U.S.C. § 794
Section 504, Rehabilitation Act
Prohibits disability discrimination by any program receiving federal financial assistance. Applies to the AG's federally funded units (Medicaid Fraud Control, etc.).
Executive Order 13166
Limited English Proficiency Access
Requires meaningful access for LEP persons to federally funded programs. Directly implicated by the Spanish-language infrastructure findings — particularly lang="en-US" on all Spanish pages.
42 U.S.C. § 2000d
Title VI, Civil Rights Act
Prohibits discrimination based on national origin in federally assisted programs. Spanish-language nameless image links and English-only No-Call path implicate this authority.
8 CCR 1501-11
Colorado Technology Accessibility
Colorado's state-level technology accessibility standards, requiring WCAG conformance for state digital services. Independent of — and in addition to — the federal DOJ rule.
05

Title II Accessibility Coordinator

CP
Designated Title II ADA Coordinator
C. Pitman
ADA Title II Coordinator · Colorado Attorney General's Office
